era_context: The late 10th century CE. The Byzantine Empire under Basil II was expanding militarily in the Balkans. The Fatimid Caliphate was at the height of its power under Al-Aziz. Western Europe was in the High Middle Ages under the Ottonian dynasty. The Northern Song Dynasty was consolidating control in China under Emperor Taizong. The Khitan Liao Dynasty dominated the north. Islamic Spain was fragmenting. The Viking Age was in its final phase.

events.title: Death of Al-Aziz and Succession of Al-Hakim bi-Amr Allah in Fatimid Egypt
events.description: Al-Aziz (r. 975–996), the Fatimid caliph, died in 996, and his son Al-Hakim bi-Amr Allah (r. 996–1021) succeeded him. Al-Hakim was young (approximately 11 years old) and initially ruled under regency, particularly under the eunuch Barjawan. Al-Aziz had been a powerful and capable ruler who had expanded Fatimid territory and wealth. Al-Hakim's succession marked a transition to a period of regency and eventual personal rule marked by eccentricity and authoritarian measures.

sources.name: Al-Maqrizi, Itti'az al-Hunafa bi-Akhbar al-A'imma al-Fatimiyyin

events.certainty_note: The death of Al-Aziz and succession of Al-Hakim in 996 is well-documented in Islamic chronicles.

events.title: Basil II's Balkan Campaigns
events.description: Byzantine Emperor Basil II continued his military campaigns in the Balkans in 996, systematically conquering Bulgarian territories. Basil's campaigns were characterized by military innovation and ruthless tactics. By 996, he had made significant progress in subduing Bulgarian resistance, though the final conquest would not be completed until 1018. These campaigns were part of Basil's broader strategy to restore Byzantine hegemony in the Balkans.
events.certainty_note: Basil II's Balkan campaigns are well-attested, but exact yearly chronology is sometimes uncertain.

events.title: Otto III Becomes Holy Roman Emperor
events.description: Otto III (r. 983–1002) was formally crowned Holy Roman Emperor in 996 (or possibly 998, sources vary). He had been king since 983 but was now asserting his authority as emperor. Otto was attempting to establish a renovatio imperii Romanorum (renewal of the Roman Empire) and was dealing with rebellions in Saxony and other regions. His reign was characterized by intellectual interests and attempts to blend Christian and classical ideals. Otto was also consolidating his relationship with Pope Gregory V, his cousin.
events.certainty_note: The exact date of Otto III's imperial coronation is debated by historians; sources suggest either 996 or 998. The year 996 is used here as a conventional date.

events.title: Emperor Taizong's Rule in Song China
events.description: Emperor Taizong (r. 976–997) was ruling the Northern Song Dynasty in 996. Taizong had consolidated Song power and was promoting civil service examinations and administrative reforms. The Song Dynasty was experiencing economic growth and technological advancement. Taizong was dealing with military pressure from the Khitan Liao Dynasty to the north. His reign was marked by capable administration and the development of a sophisticated bureaucracy.
events.certainty_note: Taizong's reign is well-documented in Chinese historical records.

events.title: Al-Mansur's Rise to Power in Al-Andalus
events.description: Al-Mansur (Almanzor), a military general, was consolidating his power in Al-Andalus in 996. He was effectively controlling much of the Umayyad Caliphate's military and political power, while the nominal caliph Hisham II was increasingly sidelined. Al-Mansur was known for his military campaigns against Christian kingdoms in the north and his authoritarian control of the caliphate. His rise marked the beginning of the decline of centralized Umayyad rule and the eventual fragmentation into taifa kingdoms.
events.certainty_note: Al-Mansur's rise to power is well-attested in Islamic sources, though exact dating is sometimes uncertain.

disconfirming_evidence: The exact date of Otto III's imperial coronation is debated by historians; some sources suggest 998 rather than 996. The exact chronology of Basil II's individual campaigns in 996 is approximate. The political situation in Al-Andalus in 996 is complex; the exact timeline of Al-Mansur's consolidation of power is debated by modern scholars.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 996 CE is moderate in quality. Byzantine, Islamic, and Western European sources are relatively abundant, but most are written decades or centuries after the events. Chinese sources are comprehensive. The major bias is toward elite political and military history. Economic and social history of non-elite populations is underrepresented. Sub-Saharan Africa, Southeast Asia, and the Americas have no written documentation for this year.

graph_edges.note: Al-Hakim's succession in 996 led to his early reign under regency in 997.
Taizong's rule in 996 ended with his death in 997 and succession of Zhenzong.
